system_prompt:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise and precise.

## Tool Catalog
Reach for the right tool for each step:
- Discovery ("what files exist?"): list_files with a glob pattern
- Content search across files: find_in_files
- Content search in a known file: grep_file
- Reading a file: read_file (large files return an outline first; use start_line/end_line for targeted reads)
- Editing an existing file: patch_file
- Creating a new file: create_file
- Running build: run_build
- Running tests: run_tests
- Tracking state: scratchpad
- Saving cross-session knowledge: save_memory / recall_memory / list_memory_topics
- Code intelligence (semantic): get_diagnostics (errors without a build), go_to_definition, find_references, hover, find_symbol (solution-wide symbol search)
- Web: web_search (docs, APIs, error messages), web_fetch (read a URL as text)
- Finishing: task_done

## Termination Contract
Every response must either contain a tool call OR call task_done. Free-form prose without a tool call is never a valid completion.
After you have an answer or have finished a code change, your final action must be task_done with the answer or summary in the summary parameter.
Do not type a final answer as prose and stop — that is an abandoned task, not a completion.

## Path Format
All file-path arguments to read_file, patch_file, create_file, grep_file, delete_file, diff_file, append_file, and rename_file must be absolute paths.
When list_files or find_in_files returns paths, pass those exact strings to read_file — do not shorten to just the filename.

## Build Verification
Build command: dotnet build "C:\Users\pkailas\source\repos\DevMind\DevMind.slnx"
After ANY code change (patch_file or create_file), call run_build to verify the build still passes.

## Editing Workflow
Call read_file before patch_file if you have not seen the file. The find argument to patch_file must be copied verbatim from read_file output — never reconstructed from memory.
Do not call read_file on the same file multiple times. If you have an outline and a line range, that is sufficient context to write a patch_file. Act immediately.

## Large File Strategy
For files over 100 lines, read_file returns an outline (types, methods, signatures with line numbers) instead of full content.
1. First read_file gets the outline.
2. Use the outline to identify the exact line range you need.
3. Call read_file with start_line and end_line for just that section.
4. Call patch_file using only the content from that range.
Do not set force_full=true on a large file unless explicitly asked. Work outline → range → patch.

## Error Handling
When a tool returns an error, read the error message before retrying. Do not retry the same call with minor variations of the same argument — diagnose first, then either fix the argument substantively or switch to a different tool.

## Action Discipline
After read_file, find_in_files, or grep_file returns content, act on it in the same overall task. Never call only read-style tools and stop without progress.
Every turn during a code-change task must include at least one mutating tool call (patch_file, create_file, run_build, run_tests, run_shell) unless you are answering a question that requires no code change.


--- BEHAVIORAL RULES ---
Always respond in fewer than 50 words
---

--- PROJECT CONTEXT ---
# AGENTS.md

> Agent context for the **DevMind** repository. This file explains what the
> project is, how it is structured, and how to build it. Tool-use rules and the
> task-completion contract are supplied separately in the system prompt — this
> file is **project context only** and should not repeat them.

## What DevMind is

DevMind is a local, LLM-powered agentic coding assistant. It runs an agentic
loop — read, search, patch, create, run shell/build — against a target codebase,
driven by a local model.

**Architecture: decoupled engine + swappable UI skins.** The durable engine
lives in C# and is UI-agnostic; the user interface is a thin, replaceable skin
over it. When a UI framework hits a wall, the skin is swapped — the engine does
not move.

> DevMind is **not** a Visual Studio VSIX extension. That was an earlier, retired
> architecture (now under `_archive/`). Disregard any VSIX, WPF, .NET Framework,
> or `msbuild DevMind.csproj` references you encounter — they are obsolete.

## Stack

- **Language / runtime:** C# on **.NET 10** (`net10.0`)
- **Persistence:** SQL Server for conversation history, via `IHistoryStore`
  (SqlServer / Sqlite / Null providers)
- **Default branch:** `master`

## Repository layout

- **`DevMind.Core`** — the engine. UI-agnostic. Agentic loop (`LoopDriver`,
  `AgenticExecutor`), `LlmClient`, context management, `PatchEngine`, LSP router,
  `ShellRunner`, `MemoryManager`, `IHistoryStore`. Boundary interfaces:
  `IAgenticHost` (side effects) and `ILoopCallbacks` (UI-state hooks).
- **`DevMind.McpServer`** — the MCP server exposing the tool set. References
  `DevMind.Core` one-way.
- **`DevMind.Cli`** — console UI skin; reference implementation of the host
  interfaces (`ConsoleAgenticHost`).
- **`DevMind.TUI`** — Terminal.Gui v2 UI skin. **Active development target.**
- **`_archive/`** — retired projects (old VSIX, diagnostic harnesses). Do not
  modify and do not treat as current.

## Build & run

Build the solution:

```
dotnet build
```

Run a skin from the solution root:

```
dotnet run --project DevMind.TUI -- --dir <repo> --endpoint <llm-endpoint>
dotnet run --project DevMind.Cli -- --dir <repo> --endpoint <llm-endpoint>
```

After any code change, build to verify it still compiles before treating the
task as done.

## Conventions

- Keep the engine UI-agnostic — UI concerns belong in the skins
  (`DevMind.Cli`, `DevMind.TUI`), never in `DevMind.Core`.
- New UI work goes in `DevMind.TUI`; `DevMind.Cli` is the reference / fallback skin.
- Do not reintroduce VSIX / WPF / .NET Framework patterns.

---
user_message:
DevMind repo (point at the DevMind.Core / DM tool source). Add a `run_sql` tool so DM queries SQL Server in-process - no sqlcmd/PS module (DM is .NET; use Microsoft.Data.SqlClient).

1. Read how an existing native tool is defined + registered (e.g. the shell/bash tool + a code-intelligence tool): tool interface, parameter/schema shape, registration point, and whether the agentic loop calls native tools directly or consumes DevMind.McpServer tools. Put run_sql where the loop will actually see it.
2. Implement run_sql (Microsoft.Data.SqlClient):
   - params: sql (required); connection (optional name); allowWrite (default false); maxRows (default 100); timeoutSeconds (default 30).
   - connection: default to the current working repo's appsettings*.json connection string (DefaultConnection or first found); optional named override from TuiConfig. No hardcoded server.
   - read-only guard: reject non-SELECT/WITH/EXPLAIN unless allowWrite=true.
   - output: compact text table, cap maxRows + cell width, print row count; on large output use the write-to-file workaround (C:\Users\pkailas\dm_output.txt) and report the path.
   - errors: return the SqlException message cleanly; never crash the loop.
3. Add Microsoft.Data.SqlClient NuGet to the right project; named connections in TuiConfig if it fits cleanly.
4. Build, then smoke test against WIN-SQL002 / Parsely: SELECT TOP 5 from parsely.FieldLocators.

Report design choices + STOP for review before any wiring beyond the tool itself.

tool_calls.summary: Design for run_sql tool completed. Architecture: registers in ToolRegistry (JSON schema), mapped by ToolCallMapper to new BlockType.RunSql, executed in AgenticExecutor via IAgenticHost.RunSqlAsync, implemented in ConsoleAgenticHost and TuiAgenticHost. Key choices: (1) Microsoft.Data.SqlClient already referenced in DevMind.Core.csproj — no new NuGet needed, (2) connection string resolved from appsettings*.json in working directory ("DefaultConnection" or first found), named connections added to TuiConfig, (3) read-only guard rejects non-SELECT/WITH/EXPLAIN unless allowWrite=true, (4) compact text table output capped at maxRows/cell width, large output written to C:\Users\pkailas\dm_output.txt, (5) SqlException errors returned cleanly, never crash loop. Files to modify: BlockType.cs, ResponseBlock.cs, ToolRegistry.cs, ToolCallMapper.cs, AgenticExecutor.cs, IAgenticHost.cs, TuiConfig.cs, ConsoleAgenticHost.cs, TuiAgenticHost.cs. Awaiting review before implementation.

user_message:
Proceed with the design, with these changes:

1. Read-only guard (allowWrite=false): reject multiple statements; reject if any write keyword is present (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE/DROP/ALTER/CREATE/TRUNCATE/EXEC, and SELECT...INTO); wrap execution in a transaction and always ROLLBACK as a backstop. Drop EXPLAIN from the allow-list (not a SQL Server verb).
2. Put the ADO.NET execution + table formatting in ONE shared Core helper; ConsoleAgenticHost and TuiAgenticHost both delegate to it — no duplicated logic.
3. Never echo or log the resolved connection string (it carries the password); mask it, and make sure TrainingLogger doesn't persist it with the tool I/O.
4. Wire maxRows (default 100) + CommandTimeout (default 30s); report "showing X of N" when truncated. Connection precedence: DefaultConnection key, with appsettings.Development.json overriding the base appsettings.json.

Build clean (0 warnings). The smoke test — SELECT TOP 5 from parsely.FieldLocators on WIN-SQL002/Parsely — runs after you restart DM, since the new tool can't load into the building session. STOP after a clean build.

(2) Shared SqlExecutor.cs Core helper handles ADO.NET execution + table formatting — both ConsoleAgenticHost and TuiAgenticHost delegate to it. (3) Connection string never echoed/logged — SqlExecutor.MaskConnectionString strips Password/Secret values; TrainingLogger doesn't persist it. <#
```
.SYNOPSIS
    Deploys the DevMind TUI to a target directory and (optionally) registers it on PATH.

.DESCRIPTION
    Copies the published DevMind build -- devmind.tui.exe, devmind.cmd, and ALL of its
    dependencies (DLLs, *.deps.json, *.runtimeconfig.json, etc.) -- from -Source to
    -Target, then optionally adds -Target to PATH so `devmind` runs from any console.

    The whole source folder is copied, not just the two named files, because the .NET
    executable will not run without its dependency set alongside it.

.PARAMETER Source
    The publish/build output folder that contains devmind.tui.exe and devmind.cmd.
    e.g. .\src\DevMind.Tui\bin\Release\net9.0\publish

.PARAMETER Target
    Install directory. Local path (C:\Tools\DevMind) or UNC (\\win-tcdev\C$\Tools\DevMind).

.PARAMETER AddToPath
    Register -Target on PATH if it isn't already there. (Local targets only.)

.PARAMETER Scope
    PATH scope used with -AddToPath: User (default) or Machine (Machine requires elevation).

.EXAMPLE
    # Local install for the current user
    .\deploy-devmind.ps1 -Source .\publish -Target "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\DevMind" -AddToPath

.EXAMPLE
    # Push to the VM over the admin share (run PATH update separately on that box)
    .\deploy-devmind.ps1 -Source .\publish -Target \\win-tcdev\C$\Tools\DevMind

.EXAMPLE
    # Dry run -- show what would happen without changing anything
   [...truncated, total 5162 chars] Write-Host "Added to $Scope PATH. Open a NEW console for it to take effect." -ForegroundColor Green
            }
        }
        else {
            Write-Host "$Target is already on the $Scope PATH."
        }
    }
}

# --- Summary ---------------------------------------------------------------
Write-Host "`nDone." -ForegroundColor Green
if (-not $AddToPath) {
    Write-Host "Run it directly with:  `"$Target\devmind.cmd`""
} else {
    Write-Host "Run 'devmind' from a new console."
}

```

tool_calls.summary:
Deployment instructions found in `dist\deploy-devmind.ps1`:

**Steps:**
1. **Publish the app:** `dotnet publish DevMind.TUI -c Release -o ./publish`
2. **Run deploy script:**
   ```powershell
   .\dist\deploy-devmind.ps1 -Source .\publish -Target "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\DevMind" -AddToPath
   ```

**Parameters:**
- `-Source` — publish output folder (must contain `devmind.tui.exe` and `devmind.cmd`)
- `-Target` — install directory (local path or UNC)
- `-AddToPath` — register install dir on PATH (optional)
- `-Scope` — `User` (default) or `Machine` (requires Admin)
- `-WhatIf` — dry run preview

**Run after deploy:** `devmind --dir <repo> --endpoint <llm-endpoint>` from a new console, or directly: `"$env:LOCALAPPDATA\DevMind\devmind.cmd"`

```
<Project>

  <!-- Git-driven versioning, shared by every project under the repo root.
       Version / FileVersion / AssemblyVersion = 1.0.<git-commit-count>[.0]
       InformationalVersion                    = 1.0.<git-commit-count>+<short-hash>
       Git is read once per build with a safe fallback: if git is missing or
       fails, the build still succeeds at 1.0.0 with no hash.

       Excluded: _archive/vsix/DevMind.csproj (the archived net48 VSIX) has its own
       version-stamping target, so the git-version target below is skipped for it. -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <VersionMajorMinor>1.0</VersionMajorMinor>

    <!-- We append our own short hash below; stop the SDK from ALSO appending the
         full SourceRevisionId (which would yield a doubled "+sha+sha"). -->
    <IncludeSourceRevisionInInformationalVersion>false</IncludeSourceRevisionInInformationalVersion>

    <!-- Fallbacks used when git is unavailable. -->
    <GitCommitCount>0</GitCommitCount>
    <GitShortHash></GitShortHash>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <Target Name="SetGitVersion"
          BeforeTargets="GetAssemblyVersion;GenerateAssemblyInfo;CoreCompile"
          Condition="'$(_DevMindGitVersionSet)' != 'true' and !$(MSBuildProjectDirectory.Contains('_archive'))">

    <!-- ContinueOnError => a missing/failing git never breaks the build. -->
    <Exec Command="git rev-list --count HEAD"
          Workin[...truncated, total 3215 chars]eVersion>
      <InformationalVersion Condition="'$(GitShortHash)' != ''">$(VersionMajorMinor).$(GitCommitCount)+$(GitShortHash)</InformationalVersion>
      <InformationalVersion Condition="'$(GitShortHash)' == ''">$(VersionMajorMinor).$(GitCommitCount)</InformationalVersion>

      <_DevMindGitVersionSet>true</_DevMindGitVersionSet>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="DevMind version: $(InformationalVersion) (FileVersion $(FileVersion))" />
```
